signals.title: USD/GBP — G8 Bilateral Theme + Fed Hawkishness
signals.text: USD/GBP at 0.7508 (GBP/USD 1.3319) — USD dominates G8 performance as DXY hits a five-week high following hawkish Fed bets and rising inflation risks. The 2.138% G8 spread confirms a strong bilateral theme; USD 3.75% vs BoE 3.75% leaves carry neutral, meaning the move is driven by growth/inflation divergence rather than yield advantage. Powell's exit and tightening global oil supplies ($101.1 WTI) further support USD safe-haven and inflation-hedge demand. Watch for a break of 1.3300 in GBP/USD to accelerate the trend.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5840 — COT LF net short -19,294 (long% 18.5%) vs retail 87% long. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence occurs as RBNZ hike bias (Next meeting 27 May) clashes with commodity-driven NZD weakness (Silver -7.84%, Soybeans retreating). Retail is aggressively fading the downtrend, creating high capitulation risk if 0.5800 is tested. Institutional shorts remain in control despite the hawkish CB bias.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Options Skew + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.75 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but options market signals caution. 25d RR at -1.73 (strong put skew) indicates heavy demand for downside protection despite JPY COT LF net short -61,340. VRP is negative (-0.3%), suggesting realized volatility is outpacing option premiums. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 18.4, but the strong RR skew suggests institutional hedging against BoJ intervention risk as price holds below 160.00.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Elevated Vol Premium
signals.text: GBP/USD IV 11.0% vs HV30 8.0% — 3.0pt VRP indicates the options market is pricing in significantly higher volatility than recently realized. This elevated premium, combined with a -1.43 25d RR (put skew), suggests aggressive hedging against further GBP downside. The bearish sentiment is reinforced by the G8 laggard status (-1.025% 1W avg). Watch for SONIA-SOFR spread widening to shift the -.
